member.name: Samantha Niblett
member.synopsis: Samantha Niblett is the Labour MP for South Derbyshire , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Neurodiversity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to invest in evidence-based training, guidance and resources to improve health and education professionals' understanding of neurodevelopmental conditions and su
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department of Health and Social Care has indicated that it will not be possible to answer this question within the usual time period. An answer is being prepared and will be provided as soon as it is available.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Neurodiversity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to support people who receive a neurodevelopmental diagnosis in adulthood, particularly women, and ensure that health professionals effectively guide people to a
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to supporting people with neurodevelopmental conditions, including women and those diagnosed in adulthood, so that they receive the right support, tailored to their individual needs, to live well in their communities.Following ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Contraceptives
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to help increase the provision of contraceptive services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government provides funding for contraception, both as an essential service which general practices (GPs) must either provide or arrange for the provision of to their patients through the GP Contract, and through the Public Health Grant to local autho...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: General Practitioners: Contracts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he has made an assessment of the adequacy of the funding uplift in the 2026/27 GP contract.
writtenQuestions.answerText: General practices (GPs) are valued independent contractors who provide £14 billion worth of National Health Services. Every year we consult with the profession about what services GPs provide, and the money providers are entitled to in return under their ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department and NHS England considered the potential impacts of expanding Advice and Guidance (A&G) as part of the policy development process, including through clinical input, established governance arrangements, and equalities considerations. A&a...

writtenQuestions.heading: General Practitioners: Telemedicine

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to improving access to general practices (GPs) by increasing capacity and making it easier for patients to contact their surgery. The changes made to online consultation systems in October 2024 are supporting greater parity acr...

writtenQuestions.answerText: Where a patient presents with a clinically urgent need, it is for the general practitioner to determine the appropriate course of action, and the patient should receive a clinically appropriate response on the same day. Where a request is ‘dealt with’, th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asbestos: Public Buildings
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, whether his Department plans to order the removal of asbestos from public (a) buildings and (b) workplaces.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Control of Asbestos Regulations 2012 (CAR) provide a robust regulatory system to ensure that asbestos-containing materials in non-domestic buildings are managed proactively, to prevent harm to building occupants on the basis that asbestos in good cond...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps her Department is taking to protect children from harm caused by AI chatbots.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have taken a power in the Crime and Policing Act to close loopholes in the Online Safety Act regarding AI chatbots. This means unregulated chatbots will have to comply with duties under the Online Safety Act to protect their users from illegal content ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Health and Safety Executive (HSE) is continuing its long-term work to deliver regulatory interventions, new communication campaigns and asbestos-related research in support of wider Government priorities. This work is aligned with those recommendation...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Imports: Asbestos
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that products imported by businesses and retailers are free of asbestos.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Under UK law, businesses placing consumer products on the market, including importers, are responsible for ensuring they are safe. The Office for Product Safety and Standards (OPSS) and local authorities have powers to require unsafe products be withdrawn...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.answerText: On 12 June 2026, I announced a one-off, time-limited meningococcal group B (MenB) immunisation programme to protect those at highest immediate risk of MenB disease this autumn.People currently in the Year 13 age group in England and Wales, those born from...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Women's Health Hubs: Derbyshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many women's health hubs have been established in NHS Derby and Derbyshire ICB; and what services they provide.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department of Health and Social Care has indicated that it will not be possible to answer this question within the usual time period. An answer is being prepared and will be provided as soon as it is available.

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Nationalisation

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government will establish Great British Railways (GBR) as a new, publicly owned body to integrate track and train. In developing these proposals, the Department has been clear that the private sector will continue to play a vital role across the rail ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.answerText: As part of the public ownership programme, existing staff at train operating companies will be able to transfer to the relevant public sector operator with their terms and conditions protected under TUPE regulations. In future, these passenger services wi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Asbestos: Sales
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of the steps taken by retailers to warn the public of sold products that contain asbestos.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Our laws are clear: the UK has a zero-tolerance approach to asbestos in consumer products. Where it is identified, businesses that supplied affected products must recall them, taking all reasonable steps to identify and notify affected consumers, and publ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Renewable Energy: Landfill

writtenQuestions.answerText: More than 300 landfill gas sites across Great Britain currently receive support through the Renewables Obligation (RO). Government is aware that the commercial viability of some of these sites may be affected when the RO support begins to expire from 2027...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero
